Name: Antonio's
Description: A fine dining institution in Tagaytay known for its exceptional steak, elegant colonial-style architecture, and lush garden setting.

Name: Bale Dutung
Description: The home of Chef Claude Tayag, serving an acclaimed degustation of authentic Kapampangan cuisine, including the famous 5-Way Lechon.

Name: Toyo Eatery
Description: A modern Filipino restaurant recognized by Asia's 50 Best Restaurants, offering innovative reinterpretations of local flavors.

Name: Basco Lighthouse
Description: A 6-story limestone lighthouse on Naidi Hill offering a panoramic view of the West Philippine Sea and Mt. Iraya.

Name: Honesty Coffee Shop
Description: A world-famous unmanned store where customers take what they need and drop their payment in a box, relying solely on honesty.

Name: Marlboro Country (Racuh a Payaman)
Description: A breathtaking communal pastureland featuring rolling hills, free-roaming cattle, and spectacular views of the Pacific Ocean.

Name: Kapurpurawan Rock Formation
Description: A stunning creamy-white limestone rock formation sculpted by oceanic and weather forces over thousands of years.

Name: Calle Crisologo
Description: A UNESCO World Heritage site featuring a cobblestone street lined with well-preserved Spanish-era ancestral houses.

Name: BenCab Museum
Description: Houses the art collection of National Artist Benedicto Cabrera, set against a backdrop of a misty organic farm and gardens.

Name: Whang-od Buscalan Tattoo
Description: The home of Apo Whang-od, the last 'mambabatok' (traditional Kalinga tattoo artist), attracting travelers seeking ancient ink.

Name: Mt. Pinatubo Crater Lake
Description: A spectacular turquoise lake formed in the crater of Mt. Pinatubo after its 1991 eruption, accessible via 4x4 and trekking.

Name: Las Casas Filipinas de Acuzar
Description: A massive heritage resort featuring a collection of restored Spanish-Filipino houses transplanted from various parts of the country.

Name: Masungi Georeserve
Description: A conservation area known for its jagged limestone karsts and the 'Sapot' (spider web) viewing platform.

Name: Enchanted Kingdom
Description: The first and only world-class theme park in the Philippines, famous for the Space Shuttle roller coaster and weekend fireworks.

Name: Taal Basilica
Description: The Minor Basilica of Saint Martin of Tours, considered the largest Catholic church in Asia, known for its baroque architecture.

Name: Mayon Volcano ATV
Description: An adrenaline-pumping adventure driving ATV quads through volcanic lava trails at the foot of the perfect-cone Mayon Volcano.

Name: Donsol Whale Shark Center
Description: The pioneering site for ethical whale shark interactions in the Philippines, allowing visitors to swim with the 'Butanding' in the wild.

Name: Intramuros Walled City
Description: The historic walled area within Manila, serving as the seat of government and political power during the Spanish colonial period.

Name: Binondo Chinatown
Description: The world's oldest Chinatown, established in 1594, famous for its authentic Hokkien cuisine, jewelry shops, and the Binondo Church.

Name: Aling Lucing's Sisig
Description: The birthplace of the modern Sisig, founded by Lucia Cunanan, credited with inventing the famous sizzling pork dish.

Name: Paoay Church
Description: A UNESCO World Heritage site known for its massive buttresses designed to withstand earthquakes, a prime example of Earthquake Baroque.

Name: Hundred Islands National Park
Description: A protected area comprising 124 islands and islets, famous for island hopping, snorkeling, and the Governor's Island viewdeck.

Name: Villa Escudero Plantations
Description: A coconut plantation and resort famous for its waterfall restaurant where guests dine with their feet submerged in the running water.

Name: Corregidor Island
Description: A historic island fortress at the entrance of Manila Bay, featuring ruins of barracks and heavy artillery from World War II.
